testSteps.description: Given I have test aggregate properties with id "test-aggregate-id", name "Test Aggregate", and version 1
And I have test passport with userId "test-user-id" and permissions ["read", "write"]
Given I have created a test aggregate
When I add a domain event with complex payload
Then the aggregate should have 1 domain event

children.description: Test Actor ensures that 1 does equal 1

testSteps.description: And the complex payload should be preserved correctly

children.description: Test Actor ensures that { message: "Complex test message", data: { id: 123, nested: { value: "nested value", array: [ 1, 2, 3 ] } }, timestamp: Date(2023-01-01T00:00:00.000Z) } does equal { message: "Complex test message", data: { id: 123, nested: { value: "nested value", array: [ 1, 2, 3 ] } }, timestamp: Date(2023-01-01T00:00:00.000Z) }

userStory.narrative:
As a domain developer
I want to use aggregate roots to manage domain and integration events
So that I can build robust domain models with event sourcing capabilities
